name: preview autolink: a bare URL in an AUTO-OPENED Markdown preview renders as a link
comment: Regression guard for bare-URL autolinking. swift-markdown does not attach the GFM autolink extension, so a URL typed as plain prose ('Visit https://example.com') is a Text node and rendered in body colour — NOT a link — while [label](url) and <url> are. This plan opens a .md whose ONLY possible link is a bare URL and asserts the user-visible outcome: an AXLink appears in the rendered web content. That is the fail-without-fix design — with autolinking removed the fixture yields ZERO AXLink, so this plan goes red. CRITICAL: like preview-copy-test.json this must NOT pass --no-auto-preview and must NOT open the preview from the View menu; both enter through the editor-visible door the user never uses and would hide any auto-preview-only defect. Cross-check: MarkdownHTMLRendererTests asserts the emitted <a href> HTML (and the negative cases — code/nested links get NO anchor); this plan asserts the rendered AXLink the user actually sees. Requires staged /tmp/medit-ap-autolink.md (uitests/stage-fixtures.sh) and the Debug build.
